articles.title: IS YOUR CRM JUST AN OVERPRICED DATABASE?
articles.summary: Most CRMs are essentially databases with a few superficial automations bolted on. But they don't have to be.

articles.title: LLMS ARE PICKING WINNERS. HERE'S HOW TO BECOME ONE
articles.summary: Posthog's traffic from LLMs grew 41x over the past two years. This converted better than almost every other channel Posthog runs. This post looks at how Posthog used Answer Engine Optimization, a mystical new field, to ensure models know about the business and its products.

articles.title: SILICON VALLEY'S NEW BUYOUT PLAYBOOK IS HITTING WALL STREET
articles.summary: Venture capital firms are buying legacy companies to integrate AI, bypassing traditional enterprise software sales. This strategy, known as the AI rollup, targets industries with low software adoption, such as healthcare and accounting. VCs like General Catalyst and Thrive Capital seek growth by permanently transforming these businesses, contrasting with traditional private equity's focus on financial engineering.

articles.title: BUILD FOR THE MARKET OF ZERO
articles.summary: NVIDIA once spent billions building a machine for a market of exactly zero customers. Its CEO told a Stanford class that this is the whole method, you reason out what the world will need and build it before anyone asks, rather than surveying customers about what they want today. His advice to founders is to reason forward to what has to be true, because customer research can only ever describe the present.

articles.title: SIZE YOUR MARKET IN GROSS PROFIT, NOT REVENUE
articles.summary: Bling Capital says the old SaaS rules for judging a startup break in the AI era, because AI margins swing from negative to 90% and a big top-down market size hides what you can actually win. The fix is to size the business by gross profit instead of revenue, working out how many customers paying how much gets you to $100M of gross profit and what slice of the market that is. They also want the core problem written out as concrete before-and-after steps with real dollar ROI, because if you can't put it on paper, you don't really understand what your product is worth.

articles.title: THE WEEKLY PLAN ALMOST NOBODY OFFERS
articles.summary: Fewer than 15% of subscription apps offer a weekly plan, yet weekly now drives 55% of all subscription-app revenue, a small slice of the field capturing most of the money. It works because a new user mostly wants to know whether it's worth risking $6 this week, and that small ask converts far better than asking them to commit $60 for the year. Adding a weekly tier takes about 20 minutes, and even the people who end up choosing monthly or annual convert better just because the weekly option is sitting there making the others look like a deal.

articles.title: WEBINAR: HOW I USED AI TO AUTOMATE SALES OPS AND SCALE TO MULTI-MILLION ARR
articles.summary: Later this month, Plane co-founder Staszek Kolarzowski (YC '17) will give a founder-to-founder webinar [11] showcasing the AI engine he built to run sales targeting, call coaching, and commissions that scaled to $Ms in ARR. Join to see what's in production and the hard-earned lessons that shaped his systems. Register to watch live or

articles.title: THE FIRST MODEL THAT PUSHES BACK
articles.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5 today, the first of the new Mythos-class models, and after five days of hard testing it's being called the best coding model in the world. The standout reaction is that it's the first model that felt like it was pushing the testers rather than the other way around, handling ambitious coding, writing, and agent work that earlier models couldn't touch. The bar for what one person can build just moved again, so the real question is what you'd attempt if the model could carry the whole build.

articles.title: AGENT TOOLKIT FOR AWS
articles.summary: The Agent Toolkit for AWS gives AI coding agents the tools, knowledge, and guardrails they need to work with AWS services. It helps AI coding agents build, deploy, and manage applications on AWS. The toolkit works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Kiro, and other coding agents developers already use. Over time, the best MCP servers, skills, and plugins from AWS Labs will be transitioned to the Agent Toolkit for AWS to ensure customers can access the broadest array of tooling and guidance for their agents.

articles.title: DASH 2026: GUIDE TO DATADOG'S NEWEST ANNOUNCEMENTS
articles.summary: Datadog's DASH keynote this year showcased the company's Bits AI support for both developer and operations workflows. It also introduced dozens of new capabilities, including autonomous detection and remediation, AI-driven release validation and testing, and unified journey monitoring. Datadog is enabling teams to build better with AI. This post details all of the major keynote announcements.

articles.title: HACKER NEWS SENT 10X THE TRAFFIC — TO THE ONE DEVICE IT COULDN'T INSTALL ON
articles.summary: Franz, a desktop app, got featured on both Hacker News and Product Hunt in the same week. Hacker News sent roughly ten times the traffic compared to Product Hunt. However, most of the traffic Hacker News sent was on mobile, while people were viewing the Product Hunt feature on laptops. Franz is a desktop app, and its site doesn't display a direct download link on mobile, so the traffic from Hacker News was less valuable.

articles.title: YOU COMMISSION THE WORK NOW
articles.summary: An early tester got access to Fable and watched it work on its own for up to nine hours on a single brief, spinning up helper agents to research, code, and check each other. The shift he describes is that he now sets the goal and judges the result, while the model handles every step in between. The power is real and so is the unease, because hundreds of choices happen in a black box you never see, so the skill that matters becomes writing a sharp brief and knowing a good result when you get one.

articles.title: REACH 7 MILLION ENGAGED TECH PROFESSIONALS
articles.summary: Ads get ignored on social media but not in TLDR! Reach developers, PMs, marketers, founders and other tech leaders where they actually

articles.title: pay attention. Learn more about sponsorship opportunities.
IN 2026, LESS THAN ONE THIRD OF GOOGLE SEARCHES STILL SEND A CLICK
articles.summary: There's been a 12.5% growth in clickless queries over the last two years due to AI features, instant answers, UI elements that keep searchers in the results, and shifting user preferences.

articles.title: GET A LIFE, IT'S A CAREER STRATEGY
articles.summary: Specialization wins in stable, fast-feedback worlds like chess and golf, but building a company is the opposite - a messy place where the rules shift and no single skill covers it.

articles.title: WHY GOOD COMPANIES GO BAD
articles.summary: A new book called Incorruptible, from the author who wrote The Lean Startup, digs into why good companies slowly go bad, drifting from serving customers to serving only the stock price.
